summary: nolimit-x: obfuscated offensive email/SMS abuse toolkit (DKIM replay, OAuth contact harvesting, SMS-gateway abuse)
details: Package self-identifies as a `red-team`/`smtp` tool and ships its entire executable surface (40+ files under.ad/) packed with obfuscator.io (RC4 string decoder, ~700-element rotated string array, self-defending regex trap, debug-protection). Deobfuscation of the bundled code shows the package is an offensive email and SMS abuse toolkit:.ad/xd.js implements a DKIMReplayService that drives a non-headless puppeteer browser to log into console.cloud.google.com / portal.azure.com with operator-supplied burner accounts, persists cookies, fetches DKIM-signed emails via IMAP (imap.gmail.com / outlook.office365.com), and replays them to arbitrary recipients through configured SMTP relays..ad/x12.js scrapes Microsoft Graph and Gmail People endpoints (/v1.0/me/people?$top=1000, /me/mailFolders/inbox/messages) to harvest contacts from stolen OAuth sessions. Additional modules implement carrier SMS-gateway abuse and open-redirect scanning. Whole-surface obfuscator.io packing of an openly red-team-labeled package is a deliberate evasion shape designed to hide hostile capability from registry scanners and to make downstream review difficult. While the abusive operations are operator-invoked (CLI subcommands rather than install hooks), the package's purpose, packaging, and hidden capabilities make it unsafe to host as a generic dependency — installation pulls a fully-obfuscated abuse toolkit into the developer's tree where any of its modules can be required and executed.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
